item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: Cruise ship put under brief quarantine after 50 passengers infected with norovirus
item.description: French authorities say a cruise ship was briefly forced to quarantine while docked in Bordeaux on Wednesday after around 50 passengers became infected with norovirus. Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: South Carolina Supreme Court overturns Alex Murdaugh murder convictions
item.description: The South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned Alex Murdaugh's double murder conviction and ordered a new trial. Murdaugh was sentenced to consecutive life sentences after being found guilty of killing his wife and son in 2021. Eva Pilgrim reports.

item.title: Trump and Xi agree Strait of Hormuz "must remain open"
item.description: President Trump and China's Xi Jinping met in Beijing on Thursday, while Israel and Lebanon are set to resume peace talks in Washington.

item.title: Breaking down main issues discussed at high-stakes summit between Trump and China's Xi
item.description: CBS News national security contributor Samantha Vinograd joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss President Trump's meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping and the top issues between the two world leaders, including the war in Iran, trade relations and the U.S. policy on Taiwan.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 14, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know first.

item.title: China's Xi warns Trump about "conflicts" if Taiwan isn't "handled properly"
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing meeting with China's President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders aiming to stabilize their trading relationship after last year's trade war.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Jury awards $49.5 million to Boeing 737 MAX crash victim's family
item.description: A jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million in damages Wednesday to the family of a 24-year-old American who perished in a 2019 Boeing 737 MAX crash​.

item.title: Rare blue-green diamond sells for record $17.3 million at auction
item.description: The 5.5-carat "Ocean Dream" was found in Central Africa in the 1990s.

item.title: CEOs worth nearly $1 trillion accompany Trump to China
item.description: The delegation of business leaders underscores the deep ties many major U.S. companies maintain with China despite years of trade tensions.

item.title: Iran insists it's ready for a new U.S. attack as Trump in China for state visit
item.description: Iran's military says it's trained and ready for any new U.S. assault as President Trump predicted a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Sneak peek: The Man with Two Names
item.description: After a conviction in the arson death of a billionaire in Monaco, Ted Maher changes his name and puts out a hit on his estranged wife in New Mexico. He denies it all. "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty reports Saturday, May 16 at 10/9c on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Feds arrest tourist accused of hurling rock at endangered seal's head
item.description: The tourist sparked outrage after a witness recorded him chucking a coconut-sized rock at "Lani," a beloved Hawaiian monk seal off a Maui beach.

item.title: National Recording Registry adds Taylor Swift, Beyoncé, Ray Charles, more
item.description: The Library of Congress revealed this year's list of 25 recordings to be preserved for future generations on the National Recording Registry.

item.title: Workers are getting paid to teach AI how to do their jobs
item.description: AI companies are recruiting a wide range of temp workers, from writers to wine enthusiasts, for hourly-paid gigs to help train their language models.

item.title: Trump demands Medicaid data for deportation. Some states go further.
item.description: Several states have required their health agencies to take on another job: verifying immigration status among Medicaid recipients and reporting them to the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Kemp calls special legislative session to redraw Georgia's congressional map
item.description: Georgia lawmakers will return to the Capitol on June 17 for a special session focused on redistricting.

item.title: Watch: Trump, Xi arrive for summit amid pomp and circumstance | Special Report
item.description: President Trump and Xi Jinping arrived for their high-stakes summit Thursday morning in Beijing. They are expected to discuss trade, Taiwan and the war with Iran. Jessi Mitchell anchors CBS News' special report.

item.title: Survivors of plane crash off Florida coast were on raft for hours, military says
item.description: The nail-biting incident took place about 80 miles east off the coast of Melbourne, Florida, which is about 175 miles north of Miami.

item.title: One-time direct cash payments can help prevent homelessness, study finds
item.description: New research from Johns Hopkins School of Public Health shows that one-time direct cash payments of, on average, $3,700 can help prevent young people from falling into homelessness. Larry Cohen, co-founder and executive director of Point Source Youth, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Videos show shots fired in Philippine Senate
item.description: Warning: Distressing video. Authorities in the Philippines tried to arrest a senator on Wednesday, resulting in a burst of gunfire in the Philippine Senate, according to an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses.

item.title: Denise Powell projected to win Democratic primary in key Nebraska district
item.description: The state's electoral system was a key issue in the 2nd Congressional District primary to replace GOP Rep. Don Bacon.

item.title: Why Taiwan is a major flashpoint between the U.S. and China
item.description: For decades, U.S. presidents have remained steadfast in their defense of the tiny Asian ally from its neighboring giant.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial
item.description: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin says ICE won't "round up" people at World Cup
item.description: Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin told CBS News that ICE arrests at the FIFA World Cup are not off the table, but the agency will not be at the global sporting event for the purpose of immigration arrests.

item.title: Lutnick testified he knew little about Epstein, his next-door neighbor
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said he was unaware for years that Jeffrey Epstein was a registered sex offender, according to a transcript of testimony released Wednesday.

item.title: New Yorker describes hantavirus quarantine experience after cruise ship outbreak
item.description: A New York native is among 16 American passengers who are quarantining in Nebraska after being on the cruise ship that is at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak.

item.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.
